ml_D_D.engine package

Submodules

ml_D_D.engine.autofill module

engine/autofill.py Auto-fill and shape inference for the node editor.

Features:

Dataset -> Input/Output shape inference Channel / feature propagation (Linear, Conv2D) Flatten -> Linear in_features calculation Inline dimension mismatch warnings

ml_D_D.engine.autofill.check_dimension_mismatches(tab: dict) → None[source]
ml_D_D.engine.autofill.infer_from_dataset() → None[source]
ml_D_D.engine.autofill.on_dataset_changed() → None[source]
ml_D_D.engine.autofill.on_link_made(tab: dict, src_ntag: str, dst_ntag: str) → None[source]
ml_D_D.engine.autofill.on_node_spawned(tab: dict) → None[source]
ml_D_D.engine.autofill.on_param_changed(tab: dict) → None[source]
ml_D_D.engine.autofill.propagate_all(tab: dict) → None[source]

Walk the model graph and: Propagate out_channels/out_features to downstream in_channels/in_features Calculate Flatten output size and fill the next Linear’s in_features

ml_D_D.engine.autofill.propagate_from_link(tab: dict, src_ntag: str, dst_ntag: str) → None[source]

Quick propagation on a single new link.

ml_D_D.engine.blocks module

blocks.py Block (node type) definitions and lookup helpers.

ml_D_D.engine.blocks.all_block_labels() → list[str][source]

Return a flat list of every block label across all sections.

ml_D_D.engine.blocks.get_block_def(label: str) → dict | None[source]

Return the block definition dict for a given label, or None.

ml_D_D.engine.generator module

generator.py PyTorch training script generator.

Takes the live graph state from all three pipeline tabs and emits a complete, runnable train.py file.

Public:

generate_pytorch() -> str full script as a string export_pytorch() file-dialog save, wired to menu

ml_D_D.engine.generator.export_pytorch() → None[source]

Open a save-file dialog and write the generated script. Wired to File > Export > Python > PyTorch.

ml_D_D.engine.generator.generate_pytorch() → str[source]

Build and return the full training script as a string. Reads live graph state from all three pipeline tabs.

ml_D_D.engine.graph module

graph.py Graph traversal and validation for all three pipeline tabs.

Data structures:

GraphNode - a fully resolved node: its tag, block label, block def, param

values read live from DearPyGui, and which pins are connected.

ValidationResult - list of Issue objects returned by validate_pipeline().

Severity is “error” (blocks training) or “warning” (advisory).

Public API

build_graph(tab) → dict[ntag -> GraphNode] topological_sort(tab) → list[GraphNode] (raises CycleError if cyclic) validate_pipeline() → ValidationResult get_tab_by_role(role) → tab dict | None

Role expectations

data_prep : must have at least one DataLoader node model : must have exactly one Input and one Output node,

all layer nodes must be reachable from Input

training : must have exactly one Loss and one Optimizer node

exception ml_D_D.engine.graph.CycleError[source]

Bases: Exception

class ml_D_D.engine.graph.GraphNode(ntag: 'str', block_label: 'str', params: 'dict[str, str]', inputs: 'list[str]', outputs: 'list[str]', connected_inputs: 'set[str]', connected_outputs: 'set[str]')[source]

add_error(msg: str, ntag: str | None = None) → None[source]

property errors: list[Issue]
issues: list[Issue]
property ok: bool
property warnings: list[Issue]
ml_D_D.engine.graph.build_graph(tab: dict) → dict[str, GraphNode][source]

Build a dict of GraphNode objects from a tab’s live node/link state. Reads current param field values and computes which pins are connected.

ml_D_D.engine.graph.get_tab_by_role(role: str) → dict | None[source]

Return the first tab with the given role, or None.

ml_D_D.engine.graph.topological_sort(tab: dict) → list[GraphNode][source]

Return nodes in execution order (sources first). Raises CycleError if the graph contains a cycle. Only includes nodes that are part of the connected graph. Isolated nodes (no connections at all) are appended at the end.

ml_D_D.engine.graph.validate_pipeline() → ValidationResult[source]

Validate all three pipeline tabs. Returns a ValidationResult — check .ok and .issues.

ml_D_D.engine.inference module

inference.py Inference mode - sample a random image from the test/val dataset, run a forward pass through the trained model, show top-k predictions.

No file uploading — samples come directly from the Data Prep graph using train=False on the dataset node.

ml_D_D.engine.inference.open_inference_window() → None[source]

ml_D_D.engine.metrics module

metrics.py Metrics window - shows a live summary of the most recent training run.

Reads directly from state.train_state which is populated by runner.py during and after training. Can be opened at any time; updates on each open to reflect the latest data.

ml_D_D.engine.metrics.open_metrics_window() → None[source]

ml_D_D.engine.run module

run.py Real PyTorch training execution in a background thread.

The thread communicates with the UI via a queue:

_result_queue — epoch results posted by the thread, drained each frame

PyTorch is required. The caller (training.py) is responsible for checking that torch is installed before calling start_real_training().

Public API:

start_training(cfg) start the background thread stop_training() signal the thread to stop pause_training() toggle pause drain_result_queue() called each frame; updates UI with results

ml_D_D.engine.run.drain_result_queue() → None[source]

Drain all pending results from the training thread and update the UI. Must be called from the main thread every frame.

ml_D_D.engine.run.is_paused() → bool[source]
ml_D_D.engine.run.pause_training() → None[source]
ml_D_D.engine.run.start_training(cfg: dict) → None[source]
ml_D_D.engine.run.stop_training() → None[source]
